{"id":"project-LICHEN-r4z2","title":"BlockReceiver silent data corruption on mismatched block sizes","description":"File: rust/lichen-coap/src/block.rs:338-357

The BlockReceiver calculates payload offset based on expected_block * self.block_size, but the actual incoming data length can differ from block_size. This causes silent data corruption when block sizes don't match:

    let offset = self.expected_block as usize * self.block_size;
    ...
    self.data[offset..offset + data.len()].copy_from_slice(data);
    self.data_len = offset + data.len();

Scenario 1 - Server sends larger blocks than requested:
- Receiver initialized with block_size=64
- Block 0: 128 bytes -> stored at 0..128, data_len=128
- Block 1: 128 bytes -> offset=64 (1*64), stored at 64..192 -> OVERWRITES block 0's data at 64..128

Scenario 2 - Server sends smaller blocks than expected:
- Receiver initialized with block_size=128
- Block 0: 64 bytes -> stored at 0..64, data_len=64
- Block 1: 64 bytes -> offset=128 (1*128), stored at 128..192 -> LEAVES GAP at 64..128 (uninitialized data!)

Impact: Silent data corruption when server doesn't match client's expected block size. While RFC 7959 says servers should respect size negotiation, a buggy or malicious server can exploit this.

Fix: Track cumulative received bytes instead of calculating offset from block number, OR validate that data.len() matches expectations.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:31:07.151777Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:31:07.151804Z"}

{"id":"project-LICHEN-r4z2","title":"BlockReceiver silent data corruption on mismatched block sizes","description":"File: rust/lichen-coap/src/block.rs:338-357

The BlockReceiver calculates payload offset based on expected_block * self.block_size, but the actual incoming data length can differ from block_size. This causes silent data corruption when block sizes don't match:

    let offset = self.expected_block as usize * self.block_size;
    ...
    self.data[offset..offset + data.len()].copy_from_slice(data);
    self.data_len = offset + data.len();

Scenario 1 - Server sends larger blocks than requested:
- Receiver initialized with block_size=64
- Block 0: 128 bytes -> stored at 0..128, data_len=128
- Block 1: 128 bytes -> offset=64 (1*64), stored at 64..192 -> OVERWRITES block 0's data at 64..128

Scenario 2 - Server sends smaller blocks than expected:
- Receiver initialized with block_size=128
- Block 0: 64 bytes -> stored at 0..64, data_len=64
- Block 1: 64 bytes -> offset=128 (1*128), stored at 128..192 -> LEAVES GAP at 64..128 (uninitialized data!)

Impact: Silent data corruption when server doesn't match client's expected block size. While RFC 7959 says servers should respect size negotiation, a buggy or malicious server can exploit this.

Fix: Track cumulative received bytes instead of calculating offset from block number, OR validate that data.len() matches expectations.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AI Agent","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:31:07.151777Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-22T20:18:42.638662298Z","started_at":"2026-07-22T20:18:42.638662298Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-22T20:23:42.638662298Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-22T20:18:42.638662298Z"}

new_value: BlockReceiver now uses cumulative data_len for offset (prevents corruption on mismatched sizes). Comment removed per style. RFC 7959 negotiation respected but robust to variance.
